title: Content-unit schema + provenance/review-log + numeric-claim record
context: wash-guides requires a machine-validated schema for every content unit so that citation coverage, review/sign-off separation, numeric-claim verification, and expert sign-off for HIGH-risk units can be enforced by CI. This schema is provisional until the content-format ADR (arch-001) lands, and its risk-classification fields depend on arch-002. It is the structural foundation all content and CI tasks build on.
objective: Define the content-unit schema (WashUnit) and ancillary records (NumericClaim, ExpertSignOff, Citation, ReviewLogEntry) that every WASH how-to must conform to, with build-time validation that rejects units missing required fields, self-approvals, HIGH-risk units without expertSignOff, unverified numeric claims, or sources with unverified licenses.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema captures all required fields: topic, riskClass, localized title and steps, audience, numericClaims[] (value/unit/context/sourceRef/verifiedBy/expertConfirmed), sources[] (org/title/url/retrievedDate/sourceLicense/licenseVerified), reviewStatus, reviewedBy, approvedBy (must differ from reviewedBy), expertSignOff (required when riskClass is high), reviewLogRef, disclaimer, lang, contentLicense, contentVersion, lastReviewed, validUntil
Build validation rejects any unit where approvedBy equals reviewedBy (no-self-approval rule enforced by code)
Build validation rejects any riskClass:high unit that lacks a recorded expertSignOff
Build validation rejects any numericClaim that is missing verifiedBy or expertConfirmed, or where expertConfirmed is false on a high-risk unit
Build validation rejects any source where licenseVerified is false
Schema follows the content-format decided in arch-001 (provisional until that ADR merges)
Schema is reviewed and approved by a maintainer and a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author
output: A committed content-unit schema definition with provenance/review-log and numeric-claim record types, plus a build-time validator that enforces all citation, review-separation, numeric-verification, and expert-sign-off rules.
